items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Hello, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Do you mind dropping by the Strand when you have time?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Umm... Brant and Bardolino are away, and they put me in charge of the welcoming proceedings.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: I'm feeling a bit nervous about it. Part of me just wants to crawl into my box... Pero is all stressed out because of me.

items.speaker: Pero
items.text: Pero, pero...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: I tend to fumble with words when talking to people... I'm worried I won't do a good job accommodating everyone.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: If the Laureate {Male=himself;Female=herself} were here, I wouldn't be so nervous. It would make things easier.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: So, {PlayerName}, as our new Laureate, we need your help.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Beyond high walls we fly

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Through mist where dangers lie

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: But with courage, we shall find

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Hope that frees the captive mind

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Il Matto?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Il Diavolo?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Truth and lies swirl in the haze

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The Laureate seeks what hides in the maze

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The devil rules from heights afar

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Where strings give way to springs of war

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Illusions rise beneath the sea

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The fool becomes the enemy

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: But bubbles burst, with gun in hand

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: To lend a light and make a stand

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The Laureate's blade clears the snare

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: A thousand stars light up the air

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Freedom blooms like flowers wide

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: As hope, the North Star, is our guide

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We already searched the ship from top to bottom. There wasn't anything that looked like a star.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: But I still want to find it.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: I want everyone to have the chance to perform Pegasus again...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: And the props we use should be items that they've all used before, things they're familiar with.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: The frequencies, or feelings, attached to the items can affect the people using them.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: They all see the star as a symbol of hope. This positive vibe will get magnified by the opera and spread to more people.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: So yeah, I'm still going to try and find it. There's always a chance, right?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: A "chance" is just another way of saying "hope."

items.speaker: Pero
items.text: Pero, pero!

items.speaker: Monster?
items.text: Roarrrr...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Uhh... Stay sharp, Pero!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'm here with you. Don't worry.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Okay.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Err... These frequencies are made up of deception and lies. They smell awful.

items.speaker: Pero
items.text: Pero, pero!

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Pero senses the "star" inside the cave and... something else she can't describe.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: We're approaching the end of this path. It seems like the center of the maze is just ahead.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Soon we'll find out what lies at the core.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: But... it may also be...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: You're right. We should keep going.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Thank you for coming, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Everyone's a bit shaken... We weren't at our best...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Not nervous, afraid.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Everyone got scared.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Most of us in the troupe have had bad run-ins with Tacet Discords. They just can't shake the fear, no matter how many times we face them. Not everyone's like you.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Pero and I weren't sure if we could keep everyone safe. But watching you in action, it hit me...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Action beats worrying any day. It's like tightrope walking. Just keep moving forward and never look down. That's the trick to staying steady.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Everyone's safe now, thanks to you. We should get these pilgrims back to the troupe before more Tacet Discords show up.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Mm-hmm, all these people are pilgrims. They've had a tough time drifting here on the Pilgrim's Sail.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Fenrico may be a phony hiding behind a mask, but I'm sure he wouldn't go back on his word as Primus.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Since you're the new Laureate, I doubt we'll see any more banishments.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: This Pilgrim's Sail probably left Ragunna before Fenrico gave his word.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: I do hope this is the last of its kind.

items.speaker: Pero
items.text: Pero, pero!

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: It's faint... but there's still hope. Pero, come help her up.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Don't worry, Pero is very gentle.
The group returns to the Troupe with Pero carrying the dying girl.

Roccia and Pero help the little girl rest in a medical tent and perform emergency treatment on her. Once her condition stabilizes, Roccia takes you to Tina to let her know you’ve returned safe and sound. Meanwhile, Pero stays behind in the tent to keep an eye on the girl.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: What took you so long? Come on, grab a big bowl of pasta while it's still steamin'.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Give it to {PlayerName}, I'll eat later.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Roccia remarks as she hurries back to the tent.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: Here. Dig in.

options.text: It can wait. I'll go see if Roccia needs anything.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text:
You accept the bowl of pasta from Tina. The homecooked flavors seem especially comforting in the moment.
Maybe it's because of all the energy you burned earlier, or maybe food just tastes better when enjoyed with company.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You put down the plate and go inside the tent to help.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: I'll come with you. I'm sure they'd appreciate the extra hand.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: I'll come with you. I'm sure they'd appreciate the extra hand.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: What?! She's still alive?

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: Wait, I didn't mean it like that! Back when we checked the ship's cabins, we thought she was already...

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: Ugh, I can't believe myself right now!

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: It's all right. We can still save her.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: We should disinfect her wounds before removing the splinters... No rush, nice and steady.
Tina and Roccia manage to treat the little girl's wounds. Her breathing gradually steadies.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: Phew... that was close.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: She's past the worst part. She'll recover soon.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: How about the others, Tina?

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: All fed and steadily recovering.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: Even though they're just kinda staring at the ground.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Well, they've been drifting at sea for so many days, and now they've washed up somewhere completely new.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: They're like a sponge that's been soaked in water. All heavy and waterlogged. It'll take a while for them to bounce back.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: What do you think we should do...?

options.text: Why don't we try talking to them?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Yes. We should see if anyone needs anything.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Everyone has been through so much...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: All those rules on how to live, forced exile, friends gone missing...

items.speaker: Pero
items.text: Pero, pero...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Is there anything we can do to cheer them up?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Well, pain relievers are a start. We've already handed those out. That'll help with the physical pain part.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: True, food is always an essential. But bellies are full, and spirits are still in the gutter.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Oh, what would I do without the Laureate around?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Medicine for the body, sure. But for the soul? We need something grand.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Everyone we talked to kept mentioning the Pegasus opera. Maybe we could arrange a little performance with them and their companions...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: It's decided then. The welcoming opera will be Pegasus.

options.text: But most of their companions are already...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Well, traditionally the troupe always puts on a show for newly rescued pilgrims.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: It's our way of saying, "Congrats, you survived!" We hope it sets the tone for something better for them.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Hmm, I know a way.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Pero and I searched through the ship and found what must be their companions' belongings.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Some are a bit damaged, but nothing I can't fix. I'll patch them up and we'll use them in the performance.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: That way, they can be together again… on stage.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: All right. Now that all the items are in place, the stage is pretty much set up.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}. I'm sure the sentiments tied to these items will make their grand entrance into everyone's hearts.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Hmm... But I feel like we're still missing something.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Maybe the stage isn't bright enough? The North Star in Pegasus is the symbol of hope after all, so it should be brighter.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: I remember Filippo said that everyone used to gather under "star" and sing, but I didn't find any stars on the ship.

options.text: Why don't we go ask him?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Yes. Maybe he can tell us more about it.

dialog.stepLabel: 询问卢卡关于星星的事
dialog.nodeDesc: 询问卢卡关于星星的事

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: I lost Mom's special treasure...

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: The light from the star kept the darkness away. It looked after everyone when we were at sea...

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: We liked to sing around it, just like Mommy did in her plays.

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: She always said the star represents hope. It would keep me safe, and protect those around me.

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: N-now I've lost it and it can't protect anyone anymore...

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: A frown begins to form on Roccia's face. She wants to comfort Luca but struggles to come up with the right words.

items.speaker: Pero
items.text: Pero, pero... Pero, pero!

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Pero says you should rest up and we'll go find the star for you.

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: B-But it's too dangerous out there... You already saved me... And Mom said I shouldn't cause so much trouble for others.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Don't worry, Luca, it's no trouble at all. You also have something important to do—rest and recover.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: And just like you said, the star watches over everyone. We're doing it for everybody.

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: Okay... I'll be good and rest.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Pero can sense the frequencies of the "star" left on Luca.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Now that Pero is on the right scent, we can start our search.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}, for that wonderful performance.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: It took everybody to make it happen. And that includes you, of course.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Since we first met, Pero and I noticed something special about your frequency. So special that even Pero couldn't figure it out.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Ever since you came to Rinascita, the misty maze has been... unraveling.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Maybe, just maybe... You can be the one to change things, no—Change the fate of the entire Solaris.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Wherever you go, things start changing. Strange, wonderful things. You're like a magnet for the unpredictable.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: I know the saying, with great power comes great responsibility. Danger will be hot on your heels wherever you go.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: So, umm... why don't you join the Troupe of Fools?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: You pulled off the perfect welcoming opera and marked a new voyage. We've been on the same side for awhile now.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: That's all right... You can join us whenever you want. It's up to you.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: If you ever find yourself in a pickle, just come find us.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: The troupe will always have your back.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: We all started out banished to this deserted island. Bit by bit, we scrounged up what we could and built our home. That's how the troupe was formed.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: We may not be rich or perfect, but at least we can sing and laugh whenever we feel like it.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: And thanks to you, we've got the chance to bring that laughter and freedom back to Ragunna.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Just like how your performance as Laureate with the Star of Hope cheered up our entire troupe.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: You're the one who helped rekindle the hope in our hearts.

dialog.stepLabel: 再次前往冥滩，寻找另一截断船
dialog.nodeDesc: 再次前往冥滩，寻找另一截断船

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: The mist...

options.text: The kind you played hide-and-seek in?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Yes. The island is covered in mist most of the year.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: But I just cleared that area not too long ago. And with the latest weather, there shouldn't be any more mist.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Battier's out fishing, like always, and our trusty "mist detector" Lario hasn't made a peep.

options.text: Maybe this mist is different from the one before?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: You're right.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Something about this feels uniquely suspicious.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Hmm, the air is so humid. It's like the mist is standing right next to us, waving hello.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: "A sea covered in veil, lay beasts of howling wail—"

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: According to Pegasus, we should be careful when there's mist close by.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: We need to find the star before it rolls in...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: This misty maze is giving me déjà vu.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Hmm... A strange and suspicious smell.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: It's familiar... but also different. Like a poorly done copy.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: In Pegasus, the maze is woven from lies and malevolence. Inside, it's as chaotic and unpredictable as a misty sea.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: There's every kind of monster in there. At the heart of it all lurks an Archdemon, who's a master of all things evil. It steals, distorts reality, spreads confusion, and plays puppet master.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: I wonder if a fellow opera fan borrowed this scene from it?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Umm, what kind of person would want to be the Archdemon?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Well, I do get why people want to be heroes. They're the stars of the show. But all this convoluted nonsense? Not exactly heroic.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: A reasonable assumption.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Whether they're playing an Archdemon or a hero, after putting so much work into this whole setup, I'm sure they're expecting something in return.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: In an opera, you can get... hope and personal growth.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Or the power to deceive and manipulate.

options.text: Only one way to find out.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: You're right. Let's go inside and see for ourselves what the mastermind behind this mess is really after.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: It's like the mist has been waiting for us.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: This isn't the usual hide-and-seek mist.

You bring the star into Luca's tent.
Luca immediately gets up from her bed as she sees the familiar glow, her face brimming with happiness.

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: The star... The star's back!

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: Oh, thank you, {Male=mister;Female=miss}! Now the star is here, I'll definitely get better!

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: I'm actually feeling much better already!

options.text: Do you mind if we borrow the star?

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: Go ahead. It's for the opera, right? I heard so much bustle outside while I was in bed. I figured they were preparing for a show.

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: The girl with the purple hair said she wanted to use something really shiny as the North Star in Pegasus. Well, nothing beats this star!

items.speaker: Luca
items.text: Now we can all sing together around the star again. It will be wonderful, just like it was back on the ship!

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Tina, your singing is as beautiful as ever. Thank you for lending us your voice for this revised version of Pegasus.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: Ahh thanks. I had to belt it out if I wanted to do Luca's shining North Star justice!

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: She can't make it up the stage, so I'll just have to bring the star to her.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Lottie Lost, here's your master's lunachord. It's missing its strings, but it can still help people.

items.speaker: Lottie Lost
items.text: Squeak... Squeak squeak...

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Diggy Duggy, this is your master's wreath. I got it patched up for you. Be sure to keep it somewhere safe.

items.speaker: Diggy Duggy
items.text: Coo coo!

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: The prop gun your master left behind, Cuddle Wuddle. It can blow bubbles now. Please take good care of it.

items.speaker: Cuddle Wuddle
items.text: Hoo hoo!

One by one, Roccia takes down the stage props and returns them to the audience. Items in hand, the audience departs feeling content. 
Only a single Luceanite is left on the stage.
